name: Chief Bey
abstract: James Hawthorne (April 17, 1913 – April 8, 2004), also known as James Hawthorne Bey and Chief Bey, was an American jazz percussionist and vocalist, a visual artist and folklorist of African traditions. He was also ordained in Nigeria as a Shango Priest.

about_quotes.speaker: Randy Weston
about_quotes.evidence: I also had Chief Bey, the great African American percussionist. Chief played African percussion and did some singing on Kofi Ghanaba... My theme song
about_quotes.doc_title: Randy Weston — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Cascadu
about_quotes.evidence: Montego Joe ... who had made it sort of on the big stage with guys like Babatunde Olatunji and, you know, Chief Bey, and you know, I mean it was that era, like the 50s, 60s, etcetera
about_quotes.doc_title: Cascadu interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, November 15, 2019
